original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory.setReferralFee Has No Upper Bound Allowing DoS of All Swaps via Arithmetic Revert
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setReferralFee and setCustomReferralFee have no upper bound checks on the referral fee value. If set to > 10000, then in Pair._update0/_update1, `_referralFee = amount * _maxRef / 10000` produces a value larger than `amount`, and `amount -= _referralFee` causes an arithmetic underflow revert in Solidity 0.8.13. This permanently DoSes all swaps on all pairs (or specific pairs for customReferralFees) until the fee is corrected.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this requires feeManager privilege, the impact is protocol-wide DoS of all trading. Even a value slightly above 10000 would cause all swap fees to revert, effectively halting the entire DEX.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setReferralFee has no require(_refFee <= 10000) check; _update0 computes amount * _maxRef / 10000 and subtracts from amount

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No upper bound on referral fee; arithmetic underflow when fee > amount
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing require(_refFee <= 10000) in both setter functions

analysis_questions: Is feeManager a multisig or EOA?
Can referral fee be set to exactly 10000 (100%) without revert to drain all fees?
What is the recovery path after such a DoS?
validation_requirements: Set referral fee > 10000 and attempt swap

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised feeManager calls setReferralFee(10001). All subsequent swaps revert in _update0/_update1 due to arithmetic underflow. All trading is halted across all pairs until feeManager corrects the value.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete protocol trading DoS; no direct fund loss but LP positions cannot be traded
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All protocol users - no trading possible
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Recoverable by feeManager setting correct value; if feeManager is compromised, requires governance intervention

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No boundary test for referral fee values

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Immediate effect on all subsequent swaps
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately observable as all swaps reverting

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
